title: Chaiturus leonuroides
intro: Chaiturus leonuroides, also known as Lemon Milfoil or Wild Chamomile, is a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include reducing inflammation, relieving anxiety and insomnia, and acting as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Chaiturus leonuroides include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Chaiturus leonuroides is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and ointments for topical application.
taxonomy: Chaiturus leonuroides is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. This plant is also known as Lion's Beard. The classification of Chaiturus leonuroides includes it being part of the order Lamiales and the class Magnoliopsida. It is native to Europe and Western Asia, and has been naturalized in other parts of the world. Its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae.
